projectDescription: A Win32 DLL plugin for Pro Evolution Soccer 6 that intercepts the game's physics integrator to dynamically rescale pass power vectors, eliminating dead-pass inertia and producing more fluid, distance-aware first-touch passing.
keyFeatures: Non-destructive JMP hook injection into the PES6 physics integrator at runtime
Dual hook system: Context Hook captures passer/receiver pointers; Power Hook intercepts and modifies EDI (pass power) before application
Distance-aware power rescaling using discrete cell distance and real Euclidean geometry
Ball inertia band classification (ZERO, VERY_LOW, LOW_MID, CARRY, HIGH) for fine-grained boost selection
Lookup-table driven boost configuration (DistanceBoostTable) fully editable at compile time
Awkward-angle rescue logic using dot-product geometry to fix passes where the ball is behind the passer
Runtime toggle via Ctrl+Shift+P hotkey with thread-safe state management
Optional Kitserver overlay integration via dynamic kload.dll binding for in-game status display
Structured debug log with per-pass metrics: coordinates, distances, EDI before/after, boost mode
Ball mass adjustment (188/198) synchronized with mod enable/disable state
publicApiSurface: ReadPassContextDistance(DWORD passer, DWORD receiver, BYTE* pX, BYTE* pY, BYTE* rX, BYTE* rY, int* dist) -> bool
ReadPassGeometryDot(DWORD passer, DWORD receiver, float* dot, float* ballDist, float* passDist, DWORD* pBallRaw, DWORD* rBallRaw) -> bool
CheckBytes(uintptr_t address, const unsigned char* expected, size_t count) -> bool
WriteJump(uintptr_t source, void* destination, size_t length) -> bool
